target.name: Shield AI
target.founder: Brandon Tseng, Ryan Tseng, Andrew Reiter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Shield AI, a Series G defense and security technology company, commands a current post-money valuation of $5.6B following $1.5B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 10 same-sector comparable companies spanning similar valuation tiers, including 8 private firms (Saronic at $9.2B, Flock Safety at $7.5B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B) and 2 public references, with a peer valuation range of $1.4B to $9.2B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a typical Series G-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.25x to the $5.6B base, implies $7.0B. Peer-median methodology across the 10-company set yields $1.9B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the defense-tech sector at comparable development stages. The median peer valuation of $1.9B sits substantially below Shield AI's current position, suggesting either a valuation premium relative to peer comparables or material heterogeneity in company fundamentals not reflected in stage alone.

The triangulated analysis yields a range of $1.6B (low) to $8.0B (high), with a median of $4.4B. Shield AI's stated $5.6B valuation sits in the upper portion of this band. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the peer set given Shield AI's later stage and larger capital base, and the degree to which sector-specific risk premiums or customer concentration justify valuation above the peer median.
